title: Selected Aesop's Fables (Vernon Jones translation)
author: Aesop
note: Eight verbatim fables stitched into one early-reader-band fixture: Crow & Pitcher, Fox & Grapes, Dog in the Manger, Shepherd's Boy & Wolf, Lion & Mouse, Hare & Tortoise, Wolf & Lamb, Town Mouse & Country Mouse. One fable per spread. Vernon Jones's 1912 translation has a longer-sentence Edwardian register that exercises the early-reader length path while staying narratively short.
syntaxNote: Syntax expectations (M5): sentenceCount and types hand-derived — 48 sentences (per-spread 4/2/2/5/9/11/7/8), all 'Wolf! wolf!' shouts and lowercase attribution tags correctly merged; types 44 declarative / 1 interrogative (how can you endure…?) / 3 imperative (Please let me go; Come, I'll run; Come with me). meanClausesPerSentence and stdev bounds are sanity bands, not full hand counts — the Edwardian register's subordination load lands ~2.0.
